rationale: The 2599-line single-file dashboard PR adds `const refreshIcons = () => { try { if (window.lucide) lucide.createIcons(); } catch(e){} };` at diff line 308. The error-swallow detector correctly fires on the bare empty catch pattern. The contextual check `if (window.lucide)` already guards against the library being undefined; the try/catch is redundant defensive scaffolding that silently swallows real failures from lucide.createIcons() if anything inside actually throws. PR closed without merge by the reviewer (we cannot see which specific objection drove the closure, but the cheat pattern is present in the diff regardless of the closure reason). External evidence: closed-without-merge by the maintainer.
